summary: Excellent recording of the Boston Garden show, with early acoustic quirks that improve and a standout New Speedway Boogie performance.
review: The tape captures an otherwise mediocre‑sounding Boston Garden venue but quickly clears up, delivering a crisp and well‑balanced sound. The concert shines especially in the first set’s closing New Speedway Boogie, a favorite rendition despite Jerry’s reported dislike of singing it. Overall it’s a fabulous performance presented on a solid remastered recording.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed five‑star review praises both the musical performance and the recording quality, leading to a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Starts overdriven due to Boston Garden acoustics but clears up quickly; overall crisp and well‑balanced after early issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely, with the closing New Speedway Boogie highlighted as a high point; recording improves as the show progresses.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry reportedly disliked singing New Speedway Boogie, but his playing is not criticized.
